question.text: Which of the following entities falls under Category-I of Foreign Portfolio Investors?

answer.text: Sovereign wealth funds and central banks

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Under SEBI (FPI) Regulations 2019, Category-I includes: (i) Government and Government related investors such as central banks, sovereign wealth funds, international organizations; (ii) Pension funds and university funds; (iii) Regulated entities such as insurance companies, banks, asset management companies; (iv) Entities from FATF member countries. **The Trap:** Family offices, corporate bodies, and charitable organisations all fall under Category-II, not Category-I. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Category-I FPIs are generally government-backed or highly regulated entities with lower risk profiles, while Category-II covers a wider range of investors.
